channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 2025 CIO Awards New Zealand: Nominations now open
item.description: Nominations are now open for the 2025 CIO Awards New Zealand. The annual awards, presented by IDC and Brightstar with support from CIO New Zealand, celebrate individuals and teams showing exceptional leadership, innovation, and foresight in their contributions to ICT and business.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta executives called ‘Careless People’ in new book from former Facebook employee-turned-whistleblower
item.description: New York (CNN) — Meta is once again facing allegations from a former employee that a growth-at-all-costs culture has caused offline harm.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Navigating the pressures facing CIOs
item.description: According to consulting firm Korn Ferry,[1] the average CIO is in their role for just 4.6 years. That’s shorter than most of their boardroom peers and significantly less than CEOs’ average tenure of 6.9 years.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Finally: some truth serum for lying genAI chatbots
item.description: Ever since OpenAI made ChatGPT available to the public in late 2022, the large language model (LLM)-based generative AI (genAI) revolution has advanced at an astonishing pace. Two years and four months ago, we had only ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Explorers discover wreckage of cargo ship that sank in Lake Superior storm more than 130 years ago
item.description: Explorers have discovered the sunken wreckage of one of the first steel cargo ships to travel the Great Lakes.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: People are paying up for this $39 yogurt that went viral on TikTok
item.description: We’re only in the third month of the year and already there have been a number of bizarre food trends go viral on TikTok—from a $19 strawberry to feeding babies spoonfuls of butter. The latest is a yogurt, called Coconut Cult, that costs $39 for a 16 ounce jar.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5060 Spotted in French Retailer's Product Listing, Raises Speculation on Blackwell Launch
item.description: Nvidia's RTX 5060 GPU has been spotted in a retailer's product listing of an Acer desktop PC, adding another rumor to the growing pile of speculation that these are the next Blackwell models to launch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Asus might have just changed the display game for good with three new air-purifying monitors - and one of them is ultrawide
item.description: Asus' new monitors may not include any significant improvements to their display quality, but the Taiwanese company is bringing enhancements to indoor air quality in a unique way.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A Nintendo Switch 2 FCC filing confirms Wi-Fi 6 and NFC support for the upcoming console
item.description: If you have any old amiibo figures lying around, it looks like the Nintendo Switch 2 will have NFC support.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft cheat sheets: Dive into Windows and Office apps
item.description: Need to get up to speed on the latest features in Excel? Wrestling with an old version of Word? Finding your way around Windows 11 or looking to get more out of Windows 10? Computerworld’s cheat sheets are easy-to-use guides to help you navigate Microsoft’s core productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Creepy humanoid robot moves using 1,000 eerie artificial muscles
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Clone Robotics’ Protoclone mimics humans with 1,000 muscles – too human for comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers urge Trump administration to cancel owl-killing plan, say it would cost too much
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers urge the Trump administration to scrap plans to kill more than 450,000 invasive barred owls in West Coast forests in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Social media might be making you sweat—literally
item.description: By now everyone knows that scrolling social media isn’t exactly good for you. But did you know it might be making you sweat? Researchers from the psychology department at Durham University tracked the physiological responses of scrolling on social media and found a rather strange side effect.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: I tried out a bunch of the AI assistants. Here’s what you need to know about each one
item.description: Does it feel to you like there are way too many AI assistants to keep track of? Between ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, DeepSeek, and others, it’s hard to remember what each one excels at—if anything.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft is developing its own AI models to compete with OpenAI
item.description: One of the hottest trends right now involves work on reasoning AI models — tools that can more or less arrive at the right conclusion on their own. (The best-known reasoning models at the moment are the Chinese Deepseek-R1, as well as o1 and o3-mini from OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New delivery robot can haul 2,200 pounds of your stuff
item.description: WeRide's unmanned electric delivery van, the Robovan W5, has 194 cubic feet of cargo space and can carry payloads up to about 2,205 pounds
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tax Season Scams: Protect Yourself with these Simple Steps
item.description: Tax season scams are on the rise, but you can protect yourself with simple steps to stay safe and avoid falling victim to these scams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
